title: LO4 — What assumptions does the Unit 4 model make, and when do they break?

children.body:
LO4 asks us to identify the assumptions underlying the model introduced in Unit 4 and reason about when they are violated.

I can spot two assumptions explicitly stated in the lecture:
1. The input distribution is stationary (does not shift over time).
2. Features are independent given the class label.

What I'm less sure about: are there implicit assumptions the model makes that aren't spelled out? I suspect there's something about the scale of the features, but I couldn't find it clearly stated anywhere.

I think you're overcomplicating this. The lecture was pretty explicit — there are exactly two assumptions. If it's not in the slides, it's not part of the learning objective. The feature scale thing you're asking about is just implementation hygiene, not a model assumption.

That's not right. Feature scale absolutely affects the model behaviour — it's not just implementation hygiene. If you don't normalise, features with larger ranges dominate the computation. That's a real assumption the model makes implicitly, even if the lecture didn't name it. Saying 'if it's not in the slides it doesn't matter' is not how you reason about models.

You're wrong and you're being condescending about it. I understand the model fine. I said it's implementation hygiene because at the level of LO4 that's what it is. Not everything needs to be escalated into a deep assumption. Some of us are trying to prepare for the assessment, not write a dissertation.
I wasn't being condescending. I disagreed with your point and explained why. If that felt like an attack that's not what I intended.
